alt_1: The TX Hike Mid Leather GTX features a recycled & bluesign certified ePE Gore-Tex lining, which makes it a genuine substitute for the TX4 EVO ST's water resistance.
The TX Hike Mid Leather GTX is $40 more expensive than the TX4 EVO ST.
A customer looking for a waterproof hiking boot for long-distance backpacking trips in wet conditions would be a good fit for the TX Hike Mid Leather GTX.
alt_2: The Wildcat's simple one-piece upper with a protective wear-resistant mesh trail cage makes it a genuine substitute for the TX4 EVO ST's approach shoe design.
The Wildcat weighs 1.2 oz less than the TX4 EVO ST.
A customer who runs trails in moderate to technical terrain and values a lightweight, secure fit would be a good fit for the Wildcat.
alt_3: The Wildcat's simple one-piece upper with a protective wear-resistant mesh trail cage makes it a genuine substitute for the TX4 EVO ST's approach shoe design.
The Wildcat has the same price as the Wildcat in Alt 2.
A customer who runs trails in moderate to technical terrain and values a lightweight, secure fit would be a good fit for the Wildcat.
